76413.subject: Claude, Codex, and our GitHub partnership
76413.body:
Hey, it's Zeno.

We've been pushing a ton of agent experience improvements, like adding an official Claude Connector https://resend.com/changelog/claude-connector, Codex Plugin https://resend.com/changelog/codex-plugin, and Remote MCP server https://resend.com/changelog/remote-mcp-server. We also started a new partnership with GitHub https://resend.com/changelog/github-secret-scanner to keep you safe. Plus, our first-ever conference, Resend Forward https://resend.com/forward, is coming.

Quick question:

> What do you wish agents could do with Resend?

Just hit reply. We read every answer.





CLAUDE, CODEX, AND REMOTE MCP

https://resend.com/changelog/remote-mcp-server

We launched our remote MCP server that works everywhere. As part of our MCP initiative, we also published an official Claude Connector https://resend.com/changelog/claude-connector and a Codex Plugin https://resend.com/changelog/codex-plugin. Use MCP with all your agents today.

→ Read the changelog https://resend.com/changelog/remote-mcp-server





NOW SUPPORTING OAUTH

https://resend.com/changelog/oauth-support

We now support OAuth connections to the Resend API. Your app opens an authorization URL, the user approves it on Resend's own consent screen, and you get scoped access tokens back. No API keys change hands, and users can revoke access at any time.

→ Read the changelog https://resend.com/changelog/oauth-support





WHAT THE NEW DMARC MEANS FOR YOU

https://resend.com/blog/the-new-dmarc-is-here

After more than a decade of real-world use and several years of drafting, DMARC has a new specification. Here's what changed, and what it means for your domain.

→ View the guide https://resend.com/blog/the-new-dmarc-is-here





OUR NEW GITHUB PARTNERSHIP

https://resend.com/changelog/github-secret-scanner

Resend is now a GitHub secret scanning partner. When a Resend API key is exposed anywhere GitHub scans (public repositories, gists, issues, and more), GitHub notifies us the instant it appears and we revoke keys automatically. This protects your domain reputation in case there's a leak.

→ Read the guide https://resend.com/changelog/github-secret-scanner





NEW VERCEL INTEGRATION


https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GjiQ0ImKXQg

Set up Resend inside Vercel's Marketplace in just a couple of minutes, including provisioning a brand new Resend team, connecting a Vercel domain, and auto-deploying API keys to your project. No DNS wrangling, no copy-pasting env variables.

→ Watch the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GjiQ0ImKXQg





MESSAGE IDS NOW INCLUDED IN SENT EMAILS

https://resend.com/changelog/message-id-for-sent-emails

We now include message_id in all email webhooks and in the retrieve and list email endpoints. This has been one of our most requested features, and it unlocks new use cases without any new primitives.

→ Read the changelog https://resend.com/changelog/message-id-for-sent

76411.subject: Vlad Tenev's X Got Hacked to Pump a Memecoin
76411.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

These are the biggest news in DeFi and crypto:

* **[Hacker behind Vladhood](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/hacker-behind-fake-vladhood-token-still-collecting-fees-after-robinhood-ceo-s-x-account-hack)**[ is still collecting fees onchain.](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/hacker-behind-fake-vladhood-token-still-collecting-fees-after-robinhood-ceo-s-x-account-hack)

* **[Uniswap](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/uniswap-adds-permissioned-pools-to-bring-regulated-assets-to-v4)**[ ships Permissioned Pools](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/uniswap-adds-permissioned-pools-to-bring-regulated-assets-to-v4)

* **[Ondo](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/ondo-s-oasis-pro-markets-cleared-to-offer-tokenized-stocks-in-us)**[ wins US clearance to sell tokenized stocks ](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/ondo-s-oasis-pro-markets-cleared-to-offer-tokenized-stocks-in-us)

* **[SEC's Peirce](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/sec-s-peirce-says-onchain-vaults-lending-can-trigger-securities-laws)**[ flags vaults and lending can trigger securities laws](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/sec-s-peirce-says-onchain-vaults-lending-can-trigger-securities-laws)

* **[KAIO](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/kaio-tokenizes-mubadala-capital-fund-across-base-solana-and-sui)**[ tokenizes a Mubadala Capital fund ](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/kaio-tokenizes-mubadala-capital-fund-across-base-solana-and-sui)

Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev's X account got hijacked on Thursday to promote a memecoin called Vladhood. The token drew more than $22 million in volume and roughly 85,000 swaps within hours. The wallet behind it had already funded itself and launched the token on Robinhood Chain 46 minutes before the hacked post went live.

Onchain records [show](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/hacker-behind-fake-vladhood-token-still-collecting-fees-after-robinhood-ceo-s-x-account-hack) the token launch and the account takeover were a single coordinated operation. The creator left liquidity locked and by press time had claimed the pool's trading fees six times, netting about 31.6 ETH plus roughly 7% of supply, and was still collecting as of press time.

It's the third memecoin-linked social hack on Robinhood Chain in three weeks, and the scams have done little to slow the network, which just [overtook Base on daily active users](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/robinhood-chain-overtakes-base-on-daily-active-users-three-weeks-after-launch). Robinhood pitched the chain for tokenized stocks; memecoins have driven its volume since day one. The memecoin trade is back in force, and the fraud has come with it.

**Read more below!**

———————————————————————————

##### **PARTNER**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/b5135e64-8585-4c4c-81e9-a6f0a7035da5/bron_banner.jpg?t=1784852130)
Caption: 

Presented by BRON

76410.subject: 🔷 The Daily: BitMEX is shutting down
76410.body:
| Robinhood's Vlad Tenev's X account hacked by memecoin hustlers | Strategy and BlackRock for Bitcoin Security Consortium | and more

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by Daniel Kuhn | Edited by Kyle Baird

Happy Thursday, Daniel here! James is out, so I'll be taking over for the next few days. *Bitcoin* is trading down slightly at $65,145, despite some positive momentum on the U.S. *Clarity Act*.

In today's newsletter, *BitMEX* said it is shutting down, *Strategy* and *BlackRock* are forming a *Bitcoin Security Consortium* , and *Robinhood's Vlad Tenev's* X account was hacked by memecoin hustlers.

Meanwhile, Clarity Act’s impact on *Circle* could be negative over the long term, Mizuho says ( https://www.theblock.co/post/409453/clarity-act-impact-on-circle-could-be-negative-over-long-term-mizuho?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ).

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*⛔ BitMEX to shut down permanently 11 years after Arthur Hayes co-founded crypto exchange*

CZ ( https://www.theblock.co/post/409495/bitmex-to-shut-down-permanently?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*BitMEX* will permanently shut down its crypto derivatives exchange on Sept. 23, ending its 11-year run after co-founder *Arthur Hayes* launched it in 2014.

* The platform, famous for popularizing 100x-leverage perpetual swaps, failed to find a buyer despite starting a sale process in February 2025 run by *Broadhaven Capital Partners*.
* The board of *HDR Global Trading Limited* , the exchange's owner and operator, decided after a strategic review of the company and wider crypto market.
* New account registrations have stopped immediately, and from Aug. 26, only reduce-only trades will be allowed, with all remaining positions force-closed before full shutdown.
* Users must withdraw assets by the deadline or face monthly fees of at least $50, subject to increases, though withdrawals will still be possible post-closure.
* The exchange reports assets exceed liabilities with zero customer funds lost to hacks.
* BitMEX pleaded guilty in 2024 to violating the *Bank Secrecy Act* over an inadequate anti-money laundering program, and was hit with an additional $100 million fine in January 2025. President Donald Trump pardoned the co-founders in March 2025.

READ FULL ARTICLE ( https://www.theblock.co/post/409495/bitmex-to-shut-down-permanently?utm_camp

Good evening, Twenty years ago this month, Marc Tarpenning and Martin Eberhard dreamed up Tesla and convinced Elon Musk to fund it. Its electric Roadster, the $100,000 sports car, kickstarted the modern EV era. Now, they’re backing TELO, a maker of pint-sized anti-Cybertruck EVs. “This goes back to Steve Jobs, who said you have to delight the customer. And that means everything about it–the look and the feel of it; the materials; the service experience; the sales experience,” Tarpenning said. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads Brendan Smialowski/AFP via Getty Images Trump Bolstered Saudi Business Connections Amid Nuclear Discussions The president’s private licensing business will launch a crown-prince-backed development in the country—a hotel, golf and residential project bearing Trump’s name. Trevor Paulhus for Forbes Meet The Wastewater Billionaire With His Eye On AI Data Centers West Texas produces lots of dirty water and cheap natural gas. That's why it's perfect for data centers, according to this billionaire.

Caption: 

# Thursday links: movies, peptides, and insiders

[Bitcoin makes cinematic history](https://www.thewrap.com/creative-content/movies/ai-movie-bitcoin-killing-satoshi-gal-gadot-casey-affleck-doug-liman/)

The makers of the to-be-released _Bitcoin: Killing Satoshi_ describe it as the first “fully-generated, studio-quality AI feature film.”

Starring Gal Gadot, Pete Davidson, and Casey Affleck, the “globe-trotting thriller” was filmed “in a gray box that could have passed for a storage facility,” _The Wrap_ reports.

“We budgeted out what it would be to do it practically and it was over $300 million,” the producer said. “It has about 200 distinct locations, from Antarctica to Antigua to Vegas, which is obviously unproducible.” 

(Why a film about Satoshi would include Antarctica is unfortunately not explained.)

Generating all 200 of those locations with AI allowed the film to be made for just $70 million. 

The performances, however, remain human — perhaps even more so than usual. 

With their surroundings left to AI, “the entire focus on set was on our performances,” Affleck told _The Wrap_. “It was much more like acting in a Broadway play than in the giant event film that Doug’s final product will actually be.” 

Affleck thinks director Doug Liman’s method of doing everything other than the acting with AI could “reinvent cinema itself.”

It’s important to note that no jobs were killed in the making of this AI movie. “Killing Satoshi” employed 107 cast members, 100 shoot crew, and 54 non-shoot crew.

Post-production will employ 55 “AI artists.”

As for the movie itself, it sounds bananas.

The official synopsis describes _Killing Satoshi_ as a “a high-stakes conspiracy thriller that asks the question no one in power wants answered: Why have the world’s most powerful and wealthiest people — Jack Dorsey, Mark Zuckerberg, Visa and virtually every major crypto organization — poured hundreds of millions of dollars into a single mega-organization called COPA, with one sole purpose: to

76407.subject: Halftime Report
76407.body:
In Context

Автор: J.P. Morgan


Автор: J.P. Morgan

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/halftime-report-jpmorgan-lapde?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BbIfu2yoLQ066ChMputzNAw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2F2GNGY9EZWck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrxwwyc7~a1-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrxwwyc7-a1
  
🌐 What are the competing forces that could define the global economy and markets in the second half of 2026? 🤝 Despite the turbulent macro backdrop, dealmaking enjoyed a blockbuster first half. Will this continue? 📈 Will the U.S. economy continue expanding over the coming months? 🔬 What’s driving momentum in healthcare M&A and capital markets? Want more content from In Context? Sign up here to receive weekly insights directly in your inbox. Mid-year market outlook: The tug of war continues The first half of 2026 was defined by elevated macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainty. However, the global expansion is on solid footing due to improving business sentiment and a resilient consumer. “We see three core themes shaping the second half of 2026. First, markets will continue to navigate the tension between the ongoing energy supply shock and a resilient growth backdrop, supported by improved labor markets. Second, the AI upstream theme will remain a key pillar of growth as the capex cycle broadens. And third, geopolitical fragmentation will persist as a structural source of uncertainty across macro and markets,” said Hussein Malik, head of Global Research at J.P. Morgan. “Against this backdrop, we see a broad realignment of supply chains, and of trade and capital flows, as security and resilience considerations increasingly take precedence over cost in global investment decisions.” J.P. Morgan Global Research also expects inflation to be sticky, resulting in uneven and gradual hikes from developed market (DM) central banks. However, the Federal Reserve (Fed) will likely be on hold for the rest of the year. Read the outlook 🔗 Global M&A mid-year outlook: Remarkable activity amid complexity The drive for growth and scale is fueling record-high dealmaking, even as national security and regulatory scrutiny redefine how transactions are structured and cleared. Meanwhile, shareholder activists are pushing for greater clarity and capital discipline. What’s the deal? 🔗 US economic growth is expected to hold steady for the rest of 2026 This forecast is underpinned by three key assumptions: consumer spending stays resilient, AI capex remains robust and the conflict in the Middle East moves toward resolution. Explore the forecast 🔗 Healthcare is ripe for M&A deals and cross-border partnerships In spite of macro uncertainty, dealmaking in healthcare remains active, with capital following
